context: Displaying a confirmation for a destructive action (delete record)
decision: Use a modal dialog
rationale: Modals block all other interaction forcing user focus; prevent accidental confirmation; easier to dismiss with Escape key
tradeoffs: Disruptive to workflow; can create modal fatigue if overused; avoid for non-destructive confirmations

context: Navigation on mobile with 4-6 top-level destinations
decision: Use bottom navigation bar
rationale: Thumb reachability zone on modern phones; matches platform conventions (iOS/Android); visually persistent status
tradeoffs: Eats 56px of vertical space; not suitable for >6 items; desktop equivalent needed (sidebar or top nav)

context: Long multi-step form (5+ fields) vs compact form
decision: Use stepped/wizard form with progress indicator
rationale: Reduces cognitive load per step; progress indicator motivates completion; easier to validate per step; reduces abandonment
tradeoffs: More clicks; user cannot see full form at once; back-navigation must be explicitly implemented

context: Showing detailed settings or configuration options
decision: Use a right-side drawer/sheet instead of modal
rationale: Drawers allow more vertical space for complex forms; user can reference underlying content; feels less disruptive
tradeoffs: Less urgency than modal; dismiss on backdrop click can lose data; mobile needs careful height management

context: Displaying a list of 10+ filterable data rows
decision: Use a data table with column sorting and filters
rationale: Tables enable comparison across rows; column sorting + filter covers >80% of user needs without custom search; familiar pattern
tradeoffs: High implementation cost; poor on mobile (need responsive strategy: cards or horizontal scroll); requires pagination strategy

context: Onboarding a new user to a complex product
decision: Use contextual tooltips + empty-state prompts instead of modal walkthrough
rationale: Users skip modal tours most of the time; contextual hints appear when relevant; empty states guide action at point of need
tradeoffs: Requires more feature flags and state tracking; harder to test; power users may find hints patronizing (add dismiss)

context: Showing system status or background process progress
decision: Use a toast notification (non-blocking) for success; blocking progress bar only for mandatory wait
rationale: Non-blocking toasts let users continue working; blocking UI only justified when action cannot be undone mid-process
tradeoffs: Toasts disappear before user reads them (min 4s); do not use toast for errors requiring action; stack management needed

context: User needs to select one option from 3-5 mutually exclusive choices
decision: Use segmented control / radio group; avoid dropdown
rationale: Dropdowns hide options requiring extra click; segmented controls show all options at once; reduce decision friction
tradeoffs: Segmented controls break at >5 options or long labels; dropdowns win for >8 options or variable label lengths

context: Displaying search results with mixed content types (articles + users + files)
decision: Use grouped search results with type icons and section headers
rationale: Grouping by type reduces cognitive scanning; users can skip irrelevant sections; section headers aid screen readers
tradeoffs: Increases result list height; sorting by relevance across groups is complex; consider tabs if type imbalance is large

context: Rich text content that needs inline editing
decision: Use click-to-edit inline pattern with visible affordance on hover
rationale: Reduces mode-switching; what-you-see-is-what-you-edit; faster for experienced users; reduces modal overhead
tradeoffs: Risk of accidental edits; must show clear edit/save/cancel states; screen readers need explicit editing mode announcement

context: Displaying price comparison across 3 tiers
decision: Use a pricing table with highlighted recommended plan
rationale: Visual hierarchy directs to best-value option; column comparison enables rational decision; recommended label anchors choice
tradeoffs: Risk of dark pattern if highlight is purely commercial not value-based; tables break on mobile (need stacked cards)

context: Error state after a failed form submission
decision: Show inline field-level errors + a summary banner at top of form
rationale: Field-level errors point directly to the problem; banner ensures users who scrolled past see something failed; screen readers announce banner first
tradeoffs: Two error locations to maintain; avoid duplicate message wording; clear errors immediately on correction (live validation)

context: Complex filtering with 8+ filter dimensions
decision: Use a filter panel (collapsible sidebar or drawer) with applied-filters chips
rationale: Chips show active filters at a glance; drawer prevents filter UI from dominating page; clear-all chip resets quickly
tradeoffs: Chips can overflow on many filters; need clear affordance to open filter panel; mobile needs sheet pattern

context: User needs to complete an action that takes 15+ seconds
decision: Show progress bar with estimated time + cancel option
rationale: Users abandon if no feedback within a few seconds; estimated time reduces anxiety; cancel option for long ops is table stakes
tradeoffs: Estimation is hard to get right; indeterminate bar is better than wrong estimate; do not show for sub-3s operations

context: Primary navigation on desktop with 7+ top-level sections
decision: Use a persistent left sidebar with collapsible groups
rationale: Vertical lists scale to many items without truncation; collapsible groups manage depth; sidebar stays visible while scrolling content (NN/g recommends visible nav for discoverability)
tradeoffs: Consumes 240-280px of horizontal space; deep nesting hurts findability; needs a collapsed icon-rail mode for narrow viewports

context: Secondary navigation within a section (3-5 sibling views)
decision: Use tabs above the content area
rationale: Tabs signal peer relationship and preserve a single context; one click to switch; ARIA APG tablist pattern gives arrow-key traversal
tradeoffs: Does not scale past ~5 tabs (overflow menu or scroll needed); not for sequential steps; tab content must be independent not hierarchical

context: Deeply nested location within a hierarchical app
decision: Show a breadcrumb trail above the page title
rationale: Breadcrumbs reveal position and enable one-click ascent; reduce reliance on browser back; low visual cost (Material 3 + NN/g)
tradeoffs: Redundant for flat structures; truncation needed on mobile; never a substitute for primary navigation

context: Optional or rarely used fields in a form
decision: Hide them behind a Show advanced disclosure
rationale: Progressive disclosure keeps the default path short and lowers perceived effort; advanced users can opt in (Refactoring UI: reduce upfront density)
tradeoffs: Hidden fields can be missed by users who need them; disclosure state must persist sensibly; extra interaction cost for power users

context: Label placement for a vertical web form
decision: Place labels above their inputs
rationale: Top labels give the shortest eye path; widest field scaling to any label length; wrap cleanly on mobile (NN/g eye-tracking)
tradeoffs: Taller form increasing scroll; less horizontal scannability than a label column; left-aligned labels can look denser for short forms

context: Collecting a value with a small known set vs a large set
decision: Radio buttons for <=5 options; native select for 6+ options
rationale: Radios expose all choices for instant comparison at low count; a select avoids overwhelming the layout at high count (Material 3 selection guidance)
tradeoffs: Radios waste space and overwhelm when the set is large; selects hide options behind a click and are slower to scan

context: Submit button state while a request is in flight
decision: Disable the button and show an inline spinner with a label
rationale: Prevents duplicate submissions and signals the system is working within the 1s feedback window; keeps the action in place (NN/g response-time limits)
tradeoffs: Disabled controls are not announced well unless aria-busy/aria-live is set; a stuck request needs a timeout and recovery path

context: Validating an email or username for availability
decision: Validate on blur rather than on every keystroke
rationale: Blur validation avoids punishing users mid-typing with premature red errors while still catching problems before submit
tradeoffs: Errors surface slightly later than live validation; a final submit-time check is still required for server-only rules

context: Empty state for a list the user can populate (no data yet)
decision: Show an illustrative empty state with one primary call-to-action
rationale: An empty screen is a teaching moment; a single clear CTA converts the void into a next step and reduces confusion (Refactoring UI)
tradeoffs: Over-designed illustrations distract from the action; must distinguish first-run empty from filtered-to-zero results

context: Empty state caused by a search or filter returning nothing
decision: Show a no-results message with the active query and a clear-filters action
rationale: Echoing the query confirms what was searched; a reset action recovers quickly and avoids a dead end distinct from first-run empty
tradeoffs: Wording must not read as an error or system failure; needs a suggestion or relaxed-query hint when matches are likely zero

context: Information density for a data-heavy analytics dashboard
decision: Default to a compact/high-density layout with an option to switch to comfortable
rationale: Expert users scan more data per screen with fewer scrolls; comfortable mode serves new or touch users (Material 3 density spec)
tradeoffs: Compact rows shrink hit areas and crowding raises error rate; persisting the user preference adds state complexity

context: Row height in a touch-first table or list
decision: Use at least a 44px touch target per interactive row element
rationale: Meets the 44px minimum so taps land reliably and reduce mis-taps on small screens (WCAG 2.5.5 / Apple HIG)
tradeoffs: Larger targets reduce rows per screen and force more scrolling; conflicts with the high-density default used on desktop pointer UIs

context: Non-critical transient success feedback (saved or copied)
decision: Use a toast/snackbar that auto-dismisses in ~4 seconds
rationale: Confirms the action without interrupting flow; auto-dismiss keeps the screen clean (Material 3 snackbar guidance)
tradeoffs: Easily missed by users not looking; inaccessible if not in an aria-live region; never use for errors that require a decision

context: Critical error that blocks the current task
decision: Use an inline alert anchored to the affected area instead of a toast
rationale: A persistent inline alert stays until resolved and sits next to the cause unlike a disappearing toast (NN/g error-message guidelines)
tradeoffs: Takes layout space and can be scrolled past if far from viewport; needs role=alert so screen readers announce it immediately

context: Confirming a low-risk easily reversible action
decision: Skip the confirm dialog and offer an Undo affordance instead
rationale: Undo removes friction for reversible actions and respects user momentum; confirmation dialogs train users to click blindly (NN/g)
tradeoffs: Undo needs a real reversal window and backend support; not viable for irreversible or externally-propagated actions

context: Searching within a large catalog or table
decision: Provide a debounced search input with results updating as you type
rationale: Incremental results shorten the path to a match and reduce typing; debouncing avoids a request per keystroke
tradeoffs: Type-ahead can feel jumpy and increases server load; needs a no-results state and accessible result-count announcements

context: Global search across an entire application
decision: Use a command-palette overlay invoked by a keyboard shortcut
rationale: A palette gives keyboard-first users fast cross-app navigation and actions without leaving the page (modern power-user pattern)
tradeoffs: Hidden behind a shortcut so discoverability is low for novices; needs a visible entry point and fuzzy-match ranking work

context: Onboarding that must collect setup data before first use
decision: Use a short guided setup wizard with a visible step count and a skip option
rationale: Sequencing required setup lowers abandonment and the step count sets expectations; skip respects users who want to explore first
tradeoffs: Forces a linear path before value is seen; every extra step costs completion so the wizard must be ruthlessly trimmed

context: Teaching a single new feature in an existing UI
decision: Use a one-time contextual coachmark dismissible and shown at point of use
rationale: Just-in-time hints are read and retained better than upfront tours because they appear where the feature lives (NN/g)
tradeoffs: Coachmarks obscure underlying UI while open; must fire only once per user and never stack multiple tips at the same time

context: Pricing page goal of nudging toward an annual plan
decision: Default the toggle to annual and show the monthly-equivalent savings
rationale: Anchoring on the higher-value plan with explicit savings frames the decision honestly and lifts annual conversion (CXL)
tradeoffs: Pre-selecting a costlier billing period risks a dark-pattern perception; the monthly option and total annual charge must stay visible

context: Free tier vs paid tiers comparison on a pricing table
decision: Lead with the recommended paid tier visually centered and badged
rationale: Center placement and a badge create a clear default that reduces choice paralysis across tiers (Refactoring UI hierarchy)
tradeoffs: A purely commercial highlight erodes trust if it is not the best value; more than three tiers reintroduces decision overload

context: Applying multiple filters on a product listing
decision: Apply filters immediately and reflect them as removable chips
rationale: Instant feedback ties cause to effect and chips make the active set and its removal obvious at a glance
tradeoffs: Each change can trigger a fetch and layout shift; on slow networks an explicit Apply button batches requests more efficiently

context: Large multi-select filter group (20+ options like brands)
decision: Make the group searchable and collapsed by default with a count badge
rationale: Search and collapse tame a long option list and the count badge surfaces how many are active without expanding it
tradeoffs: Hiding options lowers discoverability of less-common values; the search adds input handling and an empty-search state to manage

context: Same content surface on mobile vs desktop (master-detail list)
decision: Use a single full-screen view stack on mobile and a two-pane split on desktop
rationale: Narrow screens cannot host two panes so a drill-in stack fits; wide screens show list and detail together to cut navigation (Material 3 canonical layouts)
tradeoffs: Two divergent layouts double the UI states to build and test; transition logic at the breakpoint is a common source of bugs

context: Primary action placement on a mobile screen
decision: Pin the primary action to the bottom within thumb reach (a bottom bar or FAB)
rationale: Bottom placement sits in the natural thumb zone on tall phones; improves one-handed reach (Material 3 FAB / Apple HIG)
tradeoffs: A floating action button can cover content and only expresses one action; fixed bars reduce the visible content height

context: Disabling an action the user cannot yet perform
decision: Keep the control enabled and explain on attempt or show why it is disabled
rationale: A bare disabled control gives no reason and frustrates users; explaining the blocker is more helpful (NN/g on disabled buttons)
tradeoffs: Allowing the click then erroring adds an interaction round-trip; a tooltip on a disabled control is hard to reach on touch devices

context: Long table that exceeds one screen of rows
decision: Use pagination for stable deep navigation; infinite scroll only for exploratory feeds
rationale: Pagination gives addressable bookmarkable positions and a sense of bounds; infinite scroll suits casual browsing not lookup (NN/g)
tradeoffs: Pagination adds clicks and hides totals across pages; infinite scroll buries the footer and makes a specific row hard to return to

context: Asking for confirmation versus preventing the error entirely
decision: Design constraints that make the error impossible before adding a confirm step
rationale: Poka-yoke prevention beats detection: constraining inputs avoids the error class outright rather than nagging (Material 3 / NN/g)
tradeoffs: Heavy constraints can block legitimate edge cases and feel restrictive; some risky actions still need an explicit confirm as a backstop

context: Showing many status values in a dense table column
decision: Pair a color-coded badge with a text label rather than color alone
rationale: Text plus shape ensures meaning survives for color-blind users and screen readers without relying on hue alone (WCAG 1.4.1 use of color)
tradeoffs: Badges add visual noise in a dense grid; a shared legend or consistent vocabulary is needed so labels stay scannable

context: Modal that contains its own long scrollable form
decision: Lock background scroll; keep header and footer actions pinned; trap focus
rationale: Scroll-locking and a focus trap keep keyboard users inside the dialog with actions always reachable (ARIA APG dialog pattern)
tradeoffs: Focus trapping is error-prone to implement and can strand keyboard users if Escape or close is missing; very long forms may belong on a page
